key_insight: TheBioCollection turns scattered biology resources into one large, training-ready corpus for BioLMs, and using it with a fixed model more than doubles benchmark performance across biology tasks.
clinical_relevance: Better biology-focused language models could help accelerate biomedical literature understanding, hypothesis generation, and data integration for downstream drug discovery workflows.
method: Unified disparate biological resources into a 52.6B-token corpus spanning small molecules, proteins, genomic sequences, cells, and pathways.
Added tool-computed biological properties to enrich records for training.
Created TheBioCollection-Eval to test recognition, generation, and prediction across molecular, protein, genomic, cellular, and cross-domain settings.
evidence_signal: In the abstract, training the fixed Gravity-16B-A3B model on TheBioCollection more than doubled overall performance on TheBioCollection-Eval while keeping general language ability nearly intact.
watch_item: The study is benchmark-focused and corpus quality, coverage, and real-world transfer to wet-lab or clinical discovery settings still need careful validation.
paper_title: TheBioCollection: Unified Pre-Training Scale LLM Corpus for Biology
